His particular concern is maintaining the character of our villages whilst delivering high quality, appropriate and affordable houses where they are needed, and with the necessary infrastructure. He is on the Planning Policy Committee and the Planning Committee.

Dave has lived in Dormansland for the past 20 years. He is married to Debbie and has two adult children, and three grandchildren.

Before retiring, Dave worked full-time as a designer and programmer for commercial business systems, primarily in the newspaper industry, including roles at The Independent and the Financial Times.

He is now able to devote time and energy to supporting the residents of Lingfield and Crowhurst.

Dave has been a member of Lingfield Running Club for 19 years and serves as the Membership Secretary for the Victoria Sports & Social Club in Lingfield.

wards.ward: Long Ditton, Hinchley Wood & Weston Green

candidates.stmt:
My family has called Hinchley Wood home for 22 years. Both my kids went to the Hinchley Wood schools and Esher College, and during this time we've supported both schools and the local scouts. Rich - my husband - was also a coach for one of the Claygate Royals Football team.

Nearly 10 years ago, I set up the SaveHWLD Green Belt group with one of my neighbours. Since then we've fought hard with the Long Ditton Residents Association, and neighbours across Hinchley Wood and Long Ditton to protect local green spaces. In 2023 we successfully secured Village Green status for Stokes Field and One Tree Hill - protecting it for residents forever.

i'm standing as a councillor in the elections to ensure residents' voices are heard in the new East Surrey Council. You can read more in the Lib Dems manifesto.

My family and I came to Hinchley Wood over 20 years ago and have always found this a happy place to live.  I want to help keep it that way, along with Long Ditton and Weston Green, in our part of Surrey.  Our communities face increasing pressures due to tightened council budgets and over-scaled housing developments and, if elected, I aim to ensure these are not allowed to degrade our neighbourhoods.  Our homes are much more than a roof over our heads.  We need schools, shops, and surgeries.   We need water supplied, waste collected, decent roads and transport.

We need recreation and we need green space, and that is coming under increasing pressure.  I shall continue the fight to retain our Green Belt, especially that separating Surrey from London.  I want the new East Surrey Council to be effective for residents from the start, and to ensure that the proposals for promoting local democracy are much more than a gesture.

Local politics should be conducted by local people for their local residents, not by national parties with their national policies, which belong at national level.  We are not Westminster’s nursery.  Residents’ groups answer only to their own residents and have been a strong force in local government across Surrey since the 1930s.  I look forward to continuing that proud tradition.

For this I hope to have your support on 7th May.

I have lived locally with my family for over twenty years and care deeply about our community.

I am standing to protect our green spaces, strengthen local services, and support safe neighbourhoods.

I will work responsibly with residents, schools, health providers and businesses to balance growth with sustainability, good value for money, and fairness for everyone across our whole area.

Dr Caroline Kim, a lecturer at Kingston University, recently joined the St James Ward team. Caroline is a pharmacist and previously worked at Epsom and St Helier NHS trust across several departments and wards. After passing her pharmacy registration exam in 2003 and briefly working in community pharmacy, Caroline pursued further research before deciding to teach the next generation of pharmacists. She is passionate about helping people and her extensive background in pharmacology is already benefitting students.

I have been a resident in Surrey for all my life and, I'm sure like you, I'm saddened by the accelerating erosion of Green Belt.

With two children and a grandchild on the way, I really want to see the brakes applied in order to safeguard our green space and, where necessary, focus on the repurposing of long term empty commercial property to take up the demand for increased housing.

Recently retired following a career as a Certified Accountant and Management Consultant, if elected within a Reform Council, I would drive value for council tax payer's money and make sure that council tax is spent in a way that maximises value for our residents.

Rachelle has lived with her husband and teenage daughters in Thames Ditton (Weston Green) since 2010 and works in the advertising and marketing industry and is a passionate advocate of the creative industries and of future talent. She is a Trustee of a charity for the mental health and wellbeing of educational staff and mentors teenagers that have disengaged with education. Rachelle also spent a number of years as the Girls League Secretary of Molesey Juniors Football Club.

Rachelle represents the Labour Party and believes that the priorities going forward are to cut the cost of living, cut crime and cut waiting lists. On a local level it's to ensure we continue to protect our fantastic environment and provide equality of access to best in class health and education, especially mental health services and social care.

I'm married with two children and live in the East Surrey area. I'm an experienced campaigner with over 20 years striving to support local communities. I was elected as councillor for Hooley, Merstham and Netherne in 2023. I've worked hard for the community since being elected. I've found my status as councillor has given me the opportunity to influence, even though I'm an opposition councillor. I've successfully initiated a community bus service for Netherne and I'm working with campaigners to secure a full public bus service. In Merstham, I have secured an additional youth project and campaigned against the closure of shops. I'm one of 13 Green councillors on Reigate & Banstead Borough Council. More and more people are voting Green because they realise we work hard to improve quality of life for everyone.

I am standing to give local people a stronger voice, honest representation, and real action on the issues that matter most.

I am a local resident, business owner, and someone who cares deeply about the community. I have been a strong voice on the issues affecting local people, including crime, anti-social behaviour, pressure on services, and the growing frustration many residents feel at not being heard.

I believe local politics should be about putting residents first, not protecting the interests of the political class. Too many people feel ignored, taken for granted, or let down by those meant to represent them. I want to change that by being visible, approachable, and willing to stand up for local people.

My priorities include:

supporting safer streets and stronger action on crime and anti-social behaviour
protecting the character of the local area and standing up for residents
backing local businesses and defending the high street
demanding accountability from those in positions of power
focusing on practical solutions instead of empty promises

I bring real-world leadership experience, determination, and a strong belief that local people deserve honest, direct, and community-rooted representation.

I am standing because I believes residents deserve someone who will listen, speak up, and work hard on their behalf.

On election day, vote for Johnny Wolf for strong local representation and real change.

It has been my privilege to serve the community and residents in Merstham. I have been able to support many groups and charities, helping to raise both funds and awareness. I am proud to be part of the work we have started throughout our ward and look forward to continuing.

Merstham resident since 1996
Worked for the local NHS for the past 32 years Merstham
All 3 daughters locally educated
Borough councillor since 2011
Served as Deputy Mayor 2017/18

Jemma moved to the borough over a decade ago and now calls it home, alongside her husband and young son, who all use local clubs and facilities here in Merstham. A senior NHS clinician by day, Jemma has worked in education, health and youth services for many years. Jemma understands the day-to-day challenges faced by public services, with particular expertise in education, the special educational needs system and children and adults’ mental health services. Jemma wants to continue protecting the public services that benefit those most in need, whilst continuously driving quality and efficiency improvements across council services so we can look towards a sustainable future.

Graham and his family moved to this area some thirty years ago attracted by the country feel and great access to London. Two children and three grandchildren later he still sees the Borough as one of the most beautiful places and is passionate about preserving and enhancing it for future generations. His priorities are affordable housing that doesn’t destroy our inherited Green spaces, properly funded services and local transport and a real commitment to building a sustainable zero carbon and nature positive future. As a Liberal Democrat, he is standing to give voice to those who no longer feel they can endorse this current Government. He is also a committed European after decades working across Europe.

As a candidate for the Residents’ Associations in Nork and Tattenhams, I strongly believe that local government should not be a place for party politics. I have been a resident in Nork for over 50 years and will continue to work to keep Nork and Tattenhams as a great place to live, protecting our local environment, heritage and community. With my experience in both heritage and environment, including at both our borough and county councils, I look forward to continuing to keep our area independent, protect our greenbelt, parks and recreation grounds while maintaining local services to a high standard.

My priority is to maintain safer and cleaner street - tackling inconsiderate parking, speeding vehicles and noise. We must resurface roads properly, rather than patch potholes. I will ensure proper scrutiny of planning applications. The new East Surrey Council will have many challenges - council funds must be spent wisely and efficiently.

As a mother and nanny, I see every day the challenges our local families face, from the stresses of the rising cost of living to the safety of our streets.

My background in psychotherapy has taught me that real change starts with truly listening, not just reciting party lines.

I stand for Reform UK because I believe it’s time for a common-sense approach that puts the people first. I believe our hard earned money should stay in our pockets and that local services should actually work for the people who pay for them.

I’m not a career politician; I’m a neighbourly friend. I've lived in the area all of my life and care about the people and the future our children are inheriting.

Together, let’s bring some common sense back to our community!

Paul Chandler has been a councillor since May 2021. He is a Reigate resident with a passion for encouraging exercise and spending time outdoors in nature.  Paul is on a mission to help residents and improve the environment. In his spare time he organises Reigate parkrun and helps with local nature conservation and the repair cafe. He is part of a strong Green Team in Reigate and Banstead: “I've worked hard for five years representing residents. I hope to keep talking to our residents, building our community network and getting on with solving the local issues you raise with us.”

Lexi has been a member of the Liberal Democrats since 2002. Having moved to Surrey along with her husband and three teenagers she is keen to get involved with the Liberal Democrats here to support the local community. She is a keen walker and gardener. Lexi is a drama teacher in Carshalton and wants to help alleviate some of life’s dramas for the residents of Earlswood and Whitebushes.

Samantha works with local community groups to organise litter pcks and has run two picks each month for over a year. She is also a champion for the Blue Heart scheme in Horley and the residential lead for the replanting of the Upfield / Cheyne Walk roundabout. Sam had not previously considered an involvement in local poltics but has recognised the disillusionment with politics that has grown across our country. She believes we are capable of slowing sorting out the mess if we are willing to work together, and she wants to be part of the change that is needed.

Democracy only works when people have a genuine choice.

When the parties that stand make every effort to deliver on their pledges.

Yet, we have seen time and again that the Labour and Conservative parties will say one thing and then do the complete opposite.

They get away with this, because our electoral system is rigged to preserve two monolithic relics that are completely out of touch with the people of this country and the issues, we all face.

They care only about one thing: getting back into power at all costs.

When our elected leaders, who should be defending our Democracy, are telling people that voting for a smaller party is a ‘wasted vote’ then that’s got to be telling you something. Our system is fundamentally wrong, and we need real and lasting positive change so that everybody’s vote counts.

More and more people in the Sevenoaks area are quite rightly frustrated by the failure of our government to deliver on promises made in manifestos. Instead of ensuring our roads are safe and pothole-free, that we can see a doctor or dentist when we need to and that we can travel around on our public transportation system, reliably, on time and at a reasonable cost, attention seems to be on banning a type of dog and smoking.

Government and Councils are waging a war on motorists with ULEZ, LTN, fuel duties and rising taxation, disproportionately affecting hard-working people who rely on vehicles to get to work, often providing key public services.

We have an energy crisis, a housing crisis, an immigration crisis, a health crisis, an economic crisis, councils are going bust, hard work is rewarded with higher taxation so more and more people are choosing not to work. This is broken Britain and I just don’t believe the party to fix it is the same one that bankrupted the country 14 years ago.

It does not have to be this way; Reform UK is a party full of people like you and me who want to see our Country get better, not worse. Albert Einstein said that the definitio

A former healthcare market researcher and analyst, Gillian now works part-time in home energy efficiency. Her children attend the local state secondary school. Gillian says: “This community is very important to me. I have lived in the area for 21 years, raising my family here. I hope you will elect me so I can work with Ruth and Della on all the issues you raise with us."

Fredericka is the Liberal Democrat candidate for Oxted and has lived locally her whole life, building strong roots in the community. She has worked at both Wetherspoons and the Co-op, giving her a clear understanding of the day-to-day experiences of local residents and the importance of reliable services and a thriving high street. She enjoys making the most of what Oxted has to offer, from visits to the cinema and swimming pool to walks in the surrounding countryside, and has a strong appreciation for what makes the area special.

Fredericka values Oxted’s unique village feel and is committed to protecting it as the area grows and changes. She believes it is essential that roads, public services and local infrastructure keep pace with new development so that Oxted remains a place where people are proud to live. Her priority is to support a strong, connected community with well-maintained infrastructure and to ensure residents have a clear voice in the decisions that affect their town.

Will has nearly 25 years' experience working in the non-profit sector, including four years at a disabled people's charity and a decade in sexual health. His voluteer roles have ranged from transcribing historical documents to marshalling at junior parkruns. He has been a trustee of a number of community organisations, including campaign groups and performing arts and local history charities.

The new unitary council offers a fresh start and a chance to deliver high quality, cost-effective public services across East Surrey, but it will also be larger than Tandridge District Council and more remote from the people it serves. Will is keen to ensure that our communities are not neglected and will fight to see our local area gets its fair share of support and investment.

Jonathan has deep connections with Reigate, having lived in the constituency for 26 years

In 2010 Jonathan Essex was elected as the first Green Party councillor for Reigate and Banstead for the ward of Redhill East, and now leads the official opposition of 13 Green councillors. Jonathan also leads the Green Party group on Surrey County Council.

In May 2024, the Green Party received almost 25% of the vote in the Reigate and Banstead local elections across the constituency and are well placed to challenge for the Reigate parliamentary seat.

As a councillor, Jonathan has led a range of actions that have been successful, and is keen to emulate this at the national level.

Jonathan’s actions include successfully campaigning against the Biffa landfill smell in Redhill. He is also challenging the council to commit to providing genuinely affordable homes, strengthening its protection of the Green Belt, and making the case for the county council to provide the children homes and special needs school places we need in Surrey. 

Jonathan has been an active campaigner for a greener Reigate. He is a founding member of Energy Action Redhill and Reigate, and co-founder of the Surrey Climate Commission. He is also currently acting as vice-chair of the Gatwick Area Conservation Campaign. This campaign is actively opposing the increase in noise, climate damage, congestion and other impacts from the proposals to expand Gatwick to be as big as Heathrow is today. 

Jonathan passionately supports the local community across the Reigate constituency. He has helped start a community pub in Meadvale and establish a new community space on the Watercolour estate, He continues to serve as a longstanding trustee of Furnistore and of Voluntary Action Reigate and Banstead.

As your MP, Jonathan would first and foremost want to represent and successfully win campaigns in Parliament on behalf of all residents locally.

“We need to be honest about the need to rebuild trust in politics. We need to

This is a key election in which the Conservative vote is likely to be significantly lower with votes being distributed across the other parties. In my 8 years experience as an elected councillor many disillusioned voters looking for real change are voting Green. Why? We need action and investment on public services especially on health, housing, energy and water. This can be a fairer more productive country and I also support small businesses as a key to delivering this. All politicians are not the same and we have a clear vision to make a better society for future generations to enjoy.

If you do have any questions contact me on epsom.ewell@votegreen.uk

Martin has lived in Reigate and Redhill for over 25 years and has been a Councillor representing Meadvale and St John's Ward over the last three years including serving on the Overview and Scrutiny and Licensing committees.

Now semi-retired, he has spent his entire career in the music industry primarily on the live side. He has been an agent, manager, promoter, record label/magazine head, writer and government advisor - before the current government. He is best known for working as Senior Booker Advisor to the Glastonbury Festivals for over 40 years and co-founded numerous festivals and events around the world. Before he broke his leg in an accident earlier this year, he liked taking his whippet for walks in the wonderful local green spaces.

Stuart is married with two children. He lives in Redhill and has lived in the Borough all his life. He works as a senior design manager in the construction industry.

Stuart is passionate about making Redhill a better place to live, in particular, he wants to see the County Council:

Take action on the crisis in SEND provision in the county which is a major problem for many parents and carers

Tackle the large number of potholes on the roads & repair footpaths

Make walking and cycling safer by reducing urban speed limits and providing safe cycling infrastructure

Make Redhill town centre a pleasant place to go to by ensuring the pedestrian area isn’t allowed to be a car park

Improving public transport

Making more affordable housing available

If elected Stuart promises to work hard, listen, and get things done for residents of Redhill East & North Earlswood

I‘ve lived in Redhill for most of my life.  I’m now retired but I currently help out at a local food bank and I’ve previously worked as a volunteer at the Hospital and in local schools.
Reigate & Banstead is a nice area to live but I believe it could be so much better, particularly Redhill which is still very much the poor relation to Reigate. The lack of community facilities for young people particularly concerns me.  I am also very troubled by the continuing failure of SCC to improve its Children's Services and SEND provision.

I don’t like sound bites but if elected I would work hard to listen to your concerns and to push for our area to become a truly first-class place to live and work.

As a parent, I want a future where my children, and everyone else’s, can live healthy, secure lives in a fair society. I’m motivated by social justice which means building a society where everyone has genuine opportunities, where access to good health, education, housing, and security isn’t determined by wealth or background.

I've lived in the area for over 20 years and standing as a Green Party candidate feels like a natural extension of caring about the place I live and the people who live here.

I work part-time for Waitrose. This role gives me a very direct connection to everyday working life, shift patterns, physical work, and the realities many people face balancing jobs, families, and rising costs.

I will always be honest about what I can and cannot do. Politics doesn’t need more slogans or false certainty, it needs realism, integrity, and straight answers. I won’t pretend every problem has a simple fix, and I won’t promise what I can’t deliver. I will try to understand your issues properly, and do everything I can to make things better.

Outside of work and politics, I focus a lot on my friends and family, spending time with them. I follow various sports and enjoy music. I also write about things that matter to me. This can be in the form of things like the wider views on my website,I also write songs and music. I also enjoy building websites, something I've done for over 25 years.

I’m standing because I care, about people, about place, and about the future. I don’t believe in magic fixes or empty promises, and I won’t pretend to have all the answers. But I do believe in listening, in being honest, in working hard, and in representing all residents with integrity, energy, and respect.

Steve and his wife Jane moved to Redhill in the Borough 38 years ago. Steve was first elected as a Liberal Democrat Councillor to represent the residents in Meadvale and St Johns ward on the Council in 2002.

He has a keen interest in environmental issues and will promote the use of energy efficiency measures whenever possible.

Steve will continue to work closely with Borough residents, to deliver solutions to all their concerns in our local area.

Dedicated to Redhill. Experienced for East Surrey.

I have called Redhill and Reigate home for nearly 20 years, living here with my wife, Daniela, and our three children, all of whom attended our local schools in the Ward. My commitment to our community is rooted in 15 years of active local service - as a local councillor, a school governor and a founder of our local Redhill business guild. We also regularly attend church as a family and, as a member of the parochial church council, support and live by Christian values.

Professionally, I have spent two decades as a management consultant in the energy sector. This background gives me the strategic expertise needed to navigate complex transitions, which is vital as we move toward the new East Surrey Unitary Council this May.

As the borough and county authorities merge, we need proven leadership to ensure Redhill doesn’t get left behind. I am standing to represent Redhill West & Meadvale because I believe experience matters more than ever during this restructure. I know our streets, I understand our local economy, and I am ready to ensure this new authority delivers for every resident.

I’m ready to get to work for you!

Keen environmental campaigner, also campaign on disability and carer issues as I have lived experience of both. Want to see improved recycling, wildlife and more space for disabled people to move around without being hindered by pavement parking. In my free time I do bird watching, family history, art and craft, playing bowls and reading

I believe in Surrey. It is an amazing place to live, work, and raise a family. We are a county of strong communities, thriving neighbourhoods, and a resilient economy.

As your candidate for Redhill West and Meadvale, I am offering a positive vision for our county, one which will keep our community:

1. Well-run and financially disciplined 
2. An economic powerhouse
3. Prosperous and safe
4. A great place for families and residents of all ages

I believe in responsible governance. This means keeping council tax down, cutting waste, and investing in services that strengthen our community, protect the environment, and help households cope with cost-of-living pressures.

I live in Redhill West with my partner and newborn Aurora, and with a passion for representing the area for **all young working families and our children.**

**My priorities are:**

**1.** Repairing the damage Tory mis-management has caused local families that require Special Educational Needs & Disabilities (SEND) provisions. We need to properly fund these services like elsewhere around the country. Surrey Councils does not prioritise our young people - I will make sure that changes.

**2.** Finally ending the parking chaos of Redhill's pedestrian high street. Too many cars are driving and parking in what is supposed to be a safe place for people to walk, shop, and meet. If the current county council fails to act, I will push for yellow lines or a restricted parking zone in the town to finally make it safe for everyone.

**3.** I love my countryside walks with my partner, baby, and two dogs. That requires travelling to these places. So that requires; fixing potholes, prosecuting fly tippers, and promoting looking after East Surreys green spaces, large and small. From protecting our Adder populations in Mogador, to keeping grass well kept in Colesmead Playground.

A Fresh Start for East Surrey.

if Mark were elected he would press for: affordable housing; reducing speed limits in residential areas; and more recycling, particularly from home.

(Full statement to follow.)

Mike Robinson is married to Janet, with two children, and has lived in Reigate for over forty years. Mike spent ten years teaching in Surrey special schools, before working in financial services. He served over 30 years as an officer in the Army Reserve and as a governor in three Surrey schools. He enjoys working on his allotment and sailing.

I live locally and have lived in the borough for more than 20 years and worked for Reigate & Banstead and Surrey County Councils over a number of those. I still work in the public sector, now in the NHS planning services to improve population health and reduce inequalities. My children attended local schools.

I’ve joined our growing local Green Party as I believe it offers the best chance for positive. Now, more than ever, we need Green policies at the heart of everything we do. If elected, I will work with the rest of the team to achieve this and continue to serve our local community.

Having lived, worked and raised a family in the sight of our beautiful Surrey hills, have grown to love this area and feel lucky to call Surrey my home. Like many, I love our history.
I have decided to stand as a Reform candidate as I strongly believe that Reform is the only party who has the political will to reverse Britain's decline and the terrible state of affairs both locally and nationally. Without an effective opposition, my country feels like it is freefalling into chaos. We don't have to accept this.
Over the years, I have noticed a steady decline in services coupled with an increase in charges. I believe that much money is either wasted or spent on vanity projects which benefit our local community very little. I would like to see money allocated to key areas, which include cleaning our streets, correctly fixing potholes at speed, better lighting of our streets and, most importantly, proper policing of our high streets. In the past, I was able to walk home alone with no fear. This situation can be remedied with the right policies and actions.

Paul Ingram lives in Camberwell and runs an international NGO working with governments to achieve nuclear disarmament. He believes passionately in political approaches that recognise the depth of our dependency on healthy ecosystems and communities, locally and internationally. He has been an elected Green City Councillor in Oxford, and was co-leader of the Council there from 2000-02.

I am standing to give local people a stronger voice, honest representation, and real action on the issues that matter most.

As a local business owner with strong roots in the community, I understand the pressures facing residents, families, and small businesses. I see firsthand the challenges around rising costs, pressure on local services, anti-social behaviour, and the feeling many people have that they are no longer being listened to.

I believe local politics should be about serving residents, not party games. I want to be a visible, approachable, and hardworking representative who stands up for local people and speaks plainly about the changes needed.

My priorities include:

supporting safer streets and stronger local accountability
protecting the character of the area and its green spaces
backing local businesses and defending the high street
standing up for residents who feel ignored by the political establishment
pushing for practical solutions rather than empty promises

I am passionate about preserving what makes the area special while making sure it remains a great place to live, work, and raise a family.

I bring determination, real-world business experience, and a strong belief that local communities deserve representatives who will listen, care, and take action.

On election day, I ask for your support so I can put local people first.

I’m a current Reigate & Banstead Borough Councillor (elected May 2024), and if you’ve seen someone out and about sorting things, chasing issues, or asking the awkward questions… it was probably me.

Local. Vocal. Proven.

I don’t sit back and wait for things to happen. I get stuck in and make them happen. From fixing everyday issues to standing up for our green spaces, I focus on what actually matters to residents and get results.

This election isn’t about Westminster debates or national headlines. It’s about the things that affect your day-to-day life, bins, roads, planning, and support for families, and having councillors who will actually show up and get things sorted.

Being elected to the new East Surrey Council would mean I can do even more for you, with the powers to tackle the wider issues that matter to our communities.

I won’t tell you what’s best for you. I’ll ask, I’ll listen, and I’ll act, because this is our community, not mine to dictate.

I’m visible, responsive, and always available. If you message me, call me, or grab me while I’m out and about, you’ll get a real answer, not a stock response or silence.

I work closely with George Curry, and together we make a strong, effective team. If elected, we will work side by side to deliver the best possible representation for you.

If you want councillors who are active, approachable, and proven to get things done, I’d love your support.

I am not a career politician.
I am simply a hardworking, deeply disillusioned Brit , one of millions who have spent a lifetime doing the right thing.
I worked.
I built.
I employed people.
I took risks.
And over the course of that life, I have paid millions in tax believing, as most of us once did, that those contributions were helping to build a safer, stronger country for the future.

But somewhere along the way, that trust was broken.

Instead of protecting what generations before us built, successive governments have wasted and squandered vast sums of our money on failed ideas, short-sighted policies, and grand promises that delivered little except decline.

Now, as I get older, I look around at the very institutions I spent decades paying into and I see a country that no longer functions as it should.

The NHS, once the pride of Britain  is on its knees.
Police numbers have been cut while crime grows more brazen.
Infrastructure is crumbling.
Communities feel forgotten.
And families who work hard every day are being crushed under a tax burden that grows heavier while public services grow weaker.

At the same time, we are being pushed into an ideologically driven race to net zero that is driving up costs, weakening industry, and making life harder for ordinary people, all while those making the decisions remain insulated from the consequences.

And that is what angers people most.

Not just the failure.
But the detachment.

A political class that lectures the public while refusing to live by the consequences of its own decisions.

For years, like many people, I asked myself the same question:

How much longer do we simply watch this country decline and hope somebody else fixes it?

How much longer do decent people stay silent while Britain slowly loses the strength, pride and common sense that once made it the envy of the world?

For me, the answer became simple.

You either stand by and watch this country slowly disappear —
or you step forward and fight for it.

I have lived in and around the area for over 50 years.  I grew up in Tadworth and am passionate that our area retains a knowledgeable, local and experienced Councillor to represent residents views accurately and without any political party bias.  A true, unbiased voice for residents.  I have represented the area as a local Councillor for the past five years and I am keen to continue protecting the individual identities of our villages.  I remain committed to representing residents in a non-party political way, bringing our communities together whilst respecting and preserving what makes each one unique.  I offer a true, unbiased voice for residents.

Brian McGuirk is a retired Software Developer, enthusiastic about the environment.

He says: "I want to work to improve council services with emphasis on road repairs, education and a cleaner environment for everyone."

Originally from Cornwall, Helen has lived in the Banstead area for 36 years since graduating, working as an IT Programme Manager in Reigate & London after training as a Chartered Accountant. She has deep experience of the building and motivation of large and diverse teams across several continents, with a particular interest in commercial contract management and incentivisation, as well as change and risk management. She has always been active in her local Residents’ Association and her recent retirement at a time of seismic political change has deepened her determination to ensure social mobility and cohesion, with fair opportunities for all. She maintains an informed interest in the use of data and AI, which complements her passion for gardening and literature.

candidates.p: Thames Ditton / Weston Green Residents' Association

candidates.stmt: As an East Molesey Borough Councillor with deep local roots, I believe in service over politics. Having served in the Army and NHS, I bring discipline to my council work. Recently, I have focused on delivering results: improving local road safety, securing better maintenance for community amenities, and successfully challenging inappropriate developments that threaten our green spaces. Standing for the Residents’ Association means I am free from national party agendas. I don't believe in empty promises; I believe in the ‘graft’ required to maintain our area’s unique character and providing determined, independent representation for every resident in Council.

Redmond is looking for your support in becoming the elected Conservative councillor for Claygate in the May 2024 elections.

He has lived in Claygate for over 12, and before that for 15 years in Thames Ditton, he understands the local issues. Redmond recently retired from hotels & hospitality and has a wealth of experience of developing and working with people, something he can bring to his role as your community representative on Elmbridge Council.

Redmond is keen to protect the village’s unique identity, he respects and appreciates the green spaces around the village and will work to keep the green belt around Claygate. He is keen work to support the elderly in the village and assist residents with their needs as part of the Conservative team on the council.

I’m Colin Greff. I am committed to serving the Thames Ditton and East Molesey community with integrity, transparency, and dedication. I am a Thames Ditton resident and have lived in the Elmbridge area for 24 years. I understand the issues that matter most to residents—from protecting our green spaces, supporting local businesses to improving public services and infrastructure.

My name is Paul Simmons, Reform UK party candidate for Thames Ditton & East Molesey. I am a local businessman and proud father to a 12-year old autistic son, standing to tackle everyday challenges, support families and give our communities a stronger voice.

I am standing for election in Thames Ditton & East Molesey, where in my free time I can often be found along the Thames or enjoying local cafes.

Working in sustainability, I am committed to protecting our green spaces and rivers, while also supporting local businesses and keeping our high streets vibrant.

I want to ensure that this area I care about remains a great place to live, work, and spend time, and I would be grateful for your support.

The Liberal Democrats have selected Nick Dodds to stand as their candidate for Walton North in the May local elections. Nick was educated in Elmbridge and has lived in the borough for the last 8 years.

He is a TV sports journalist and producer and will use his communication skills to speak powerfully for the people of Walton North. He has been active locally, and further afield, in the Lib Dems since the 2019 General Election campaign, driven by a commitment to improve local democracy and to reflect the strengths of the local community.

Nick knows how important it is to listen to local people and understand the kind of change they want to see in Walton North to make it a better place to live.

Nick says: “Local democracy is one of the primary reasons I am Liberal Democrat. I want to be an advocate for local people and the issues that matter to them. From education, health and social care, housing and climate change. I want to help empower local people to make them feel democracy works for them.

“To improve the voice of local people, more Liberal Democrat voices are needed on the local council. If elected, I will seek a constant dialogue with residents and, as their Councillor, provide effective and sympathetic representation.”

Nick Dodds's priorities - Protecting the Green Belt and preserving the character of Walton - Campaigning for better public transport - Supporting local businesses

Gregor Macgregor works for a US company and was brought up in Camberley. He has been an Elmbridge Councillor since 2023.

He has led the campaign to save the Walton Hospital and Bowls Club. He has gained 4,500 signatures against the plans of the local NHS Alliance.

He is standing as an Independent candidate. He believes that politics should not enter Local Government, having seen the harmful effects of Political Parties taking over councils.

He hopes to see the revival of Walton Town Centre, resurfaced roads and the Thames Towpath being a place where people can feel safe to walk.

I am a full-time financial services professional, wife, and mother of two grown-up children. I am committed to supporting our community in Walton and will challenge the council's fiscal decisions to ensure public funds are used to finance essential services and initiatives that deliver real benefits to residents with a focus on transparency and accountability.

My name is Lloyd and I am standing as your Conservative candidate in Walton-on-Thames. 

I have lived in Walton for several years and work as a Business Consultant in London.

I am standing because I care about the community in Walton and want to make a difference. I have the energy and determination to stand up for our community, to work tirelessly with colleagues in the Borough and County Councils as we tackle issues like anti-social behaviour.

Let's be clear about what this election on Thursday 4th May means. It's about who we want to represent Walton. This is a local election, about local issues. This is about how hard your councillor is willing to work on behalf of our community. 

I would be honoured to represent the people of Walton, and I would absolutely give it my all.

I've lived in Elmbridge my entire life, growing up in Lower Green, Esher, before moving to Walton in my teens.
I've worked in hospitality for the last 10 years, and have heard and seen first hand the struggles and fears everyday people have and experience throughout the borough.
Having been a volunteer youth worker since I was 16, and have been keenly focused on delivering education for peace, and how to deliver a sustainable future with young people aged 6 up to 20, and over the last few years I've stepped up to a national governance role withing the organisation that delivers that, the Woodcraft Folk, helping further that delivery across the country. 
I'm keen to see an improvement to the future of all young people in Surrey

I'm Kirsty Hewens - your Liberal Democrat candidate in Walton South and Oatlands in the elections on May 7th. I love living in Walton and have enjoyed raising my family here. I know how much people value our local community, which is why it was an honour to be elected in 2022 to represent Walton South on Elmbridge Borough Council.
Since being elected, I have served on several committees and, since May 2024, have been Cabinet Member for Local Economy, Car Parking, Enterprise and Community Safety. In this role, I introduced 30 minutes of free parking in village car parks and extended this in Walton town centre — helping to support local businesses.
I am proud that Walton town centre secured a second term as a Business Improvement District, alongside new BIDs in Cobham, Weybridge and Esher, strengthening our town centres and supporting regeneration.
Like many residents, I am concerned about the rising cost of living. As a mum and a long-standing foodbank volunteer, I am committed to supporting families, protecting vulnerable residents, and ensuring children’s services, adult social care and care for older people are properly supported.
I have lived in Walton for many years and have always been actively involved in the community. It was this commitment that led me to stand for election in 2022, and why I am standing again in May 2026 for the new East Surrey unitary authority.
I would be honoured to represent Walton and Oatlands and to be a strong, hardworking voice for our community.

Ashley has lived in Weybridge with his family for over twenty years after moving here to teach physics at a local secondary school. He is an active rower and orienteer. He enjoys cycling and sings in a local choir. An Elmbridge Borough Councillor since 2019 and a cabinet member since 2021, he is proud of the number of improvements in Weybridge he has proposed that have been implemented: improvements to the town paths, including installing lighting and better access through the main car park, his idea to install a sustainable splash pad which was hugely popular when it opened last year and his suggested alterations to the refurbished Library for a landscaped seating area and public entrance from the car park. 
Elected as the Surrey councillor for Walton South & Oatlands in 2023, he has been successful in improving safety outside schools: yellow zig zag lines, 20 mph restrictions and wider crossing refuges. He will continue to  push for more zebra crossings and safer streets for walking, wheeling and cycling.

My background is in chemistry and geology, and  now I'm a proud Ambassador for the Abolition of Modern Slavery. I facilitate a team of 30 other ambassadors across Surrey and Sussex.

I attend church every Sunday and I work with young people to give them confidence to stand up and read in front of a big audience from the lectern. It's a very fulfilling role.

I've lived in Walton for over 20 years and seen it grow from a bustling village to a struggling town that needs support. I hope to be able to give it that support. I'm very big advocate of the local hospital services and I want to stop the Lib Dem plans to stop funding Community Centres and Meals on Wheels within the borough.

I've worked in communications, marketing and branding for over 20 years, having led teams at some of the UK’s most well-known transport brands, including British Airways, Virgin Trains, and London North Eastern Railway.

I'm a proud trustee of a gay right’s charity, LGB Alliance, which advocates for sex-based rights and also intervened in the Supreme Court ruling of what is a woman. I've also sat on the Citizens Advice People Committee as an independent adviser.

Having lived in Weybridge and now Walton, I can see what happens when one town thrives and another doesn't. I really want local businesses to do well, and expand free car parking to help them. I oppose anti-motorist measures such as blanket 20mph zones and am keen to enforce the HGV ban which is causing problems especially for Oatlands residents. I'm also committed to reversing Lib Dem plans to withdraw support for Community Centres.

I consider myself to be a no-nonsense Conservative, opposing vanity projects, identity politics and keen for common-sense to prevail!

I have lived, worked and raised two boys in the community.

I welcome the opportunity to represent the needs and issues for the residents of Walton South & Oatlands such as anti-social behaviour in the town centre, potential 20 mph zones and potholes.

Let's secure a fair deal for LOCAL residents. Your concerns are mine too.

Like many others in Reform I have been inspired to stand as a candidate because I am passionate about making a real difference and being part of the solution.

I hope to bring a fresh perspective and use my experience in service delivery management to drive results.

Andrew says:  “We're lucky in Elmbridge to have a strong group of independent and resident association councillors who can act in the best interest of residents without party politics getting in the way.  ​ As an Independent I will always put you and the area we all call home first.  ​ I would be honoured to represent Walton South & Oatlands ward on the new East Surrey Council and commit to be a hard-working, independent voice for all residents.” ​

Andrew will:  - Bring a strong track record as an Elmbridge councillor and use that experience for your benefit  - Listen to you and energetically tackle your local concerns and priorities - As an Independent, Andrew will be free to focus on what matters to you and make decisions with the best interests of all residents in mind​

Andrew has lived in Walton all his life and looks forward to adding to his track record of eleven years’ previous experience as an Elmbridge Borough councillor.

During that time he worked to improve our borough, from enhancing local facilities to fighting inappropriate development, and helped residents resolve issues confronting them. Andrew sat on all the main council committees and served as Cabinet Member for Planning.  With this experience he will be able to get stuck in straight away helping residents in our ward.

Andrew has previously served on the Local Citizens’ Advice Bureau Trustee Board and the Cecil Hepworth Playhouse Users’ Committee. Outside of work and local politics, Andrew is a passionate and very frequent theatregoer.

After 14 long years of dire Tory negligence and incompetence, Labour has begun the hard work of getting the country back on its feet. Lots has been achieved already — particularly on improving the NHS and tackling immigration problems. But lots more remains to be done. And it will be. Labour is the only credible party ( unlike the fantasists from Green and Reform) for these big jobs — both nationally and locally. And unlike the Lib Dems or the Resident / Independent groups, who are only found in small localised pockets, Labour has representation, experience and strong infrastructure throughout the country to carry through the big changes and improvements that are needed.

As a Tatsfield working mum, I know the pressures families & local businesses are under. Too often we are ignored by the bureaucratic system. I want to be your local representative who listens and delivers. 
Let's: 
- save our green belt
- fix potholes
- improve services
- tackle crime 
- get value for money!

Perry moved to Warlingham over 24 years ago, having spent over 20 years working In Switzerland for blue chip pharmaceutical companies. Perry has extensive business experience and particular expertise in change management. He has served as a Governor at Warlingham Village Primary School and as a District Councillor for Warlingham East, Chelsham and Farleigh since 2022, where he sits on the Planning and Strategy & Resources committees. He is also Vice Chair of Warlingham Parish Council, actively contributing to its activities including sitting on the Warlingham Green Improvement committee.

He is very keen to contribute his expertise to the new East Surrey Unitary Council especially in financial management and improving accountability and efficiency. With new proposals from central government which will affect Surrey, he believes his expertise and local knowledge will be very valuable in shaping strategies for Social Care.

He is also keen to continue to fight inappropriate planning developments which threaten the attractive character of our villages and countryside, protect and improve community facilities like Warlingham Library and the Bond Road Tip, and support local businesses in Warlingham, Woldingham and Tatsfield.

Jeremy has lived in the area for fifteen years, settling down and raising a family. By day, he works in the TV industry, building everything from apps to control rooms and studios. Between work and driving the “Dad Taxi,” Jeremy enjoys exercising in Nonsuch Park and cycling around the local area. Jeremy is committed to being, if elected, a practical, focused councillor, working to bring meaningful improvements that make a real difference to daily life in West Ewell. To put it simply, he believes that sensible, everyday people should have a voice in local decision-making, helping ensure the community thrives.

Sammi was raised on Heatherside Road, married just around the corner at All Saints Church, and now has children in local childcare - a true West Ewell local. Now working as a Sign Language Interpreter, Sammi is passionate about building an inclusive community that is welcoming for all, and she is particularly committed to improving SEND provision so that every child is given the opportunity to thrive. Having grown up in West Ewell herself, she understands the everyday issues that matter to local residents and actually cares about finding solutions. Sammi is motivated by a desire to show her children that fairness, sensibility and collaborative leadership can achieve real progress. When she's not working, you'll often find Sammi and her husband at Bourne Hall, keeping up with their two children and enjoying time together as a family.

candidates.p: Residents Associations of Epsom and Ewell
Residents Associations of Epsom and Ewell
candidates.stmt: Neil has lived in the area for over 50 years. His working career was in IT as a Senior Manager and Project Manager before starting his own company based in Epsom. Neil is married and has six grown-up children and four grandchildren. He has been a very active and effective Residents' Association Borough Councillor representing Town Ward on Epsom & Ewell Borough Council since 2007. Because he lives here in the Epsom West Division Neil is aware of local issues and is passionate about all areas of Epsom. He understands what needs to be done and speaks out on behalf of residents. Importantly he is easily accessible to all our residents via telephone, email and personal contact. Neil has been involved with Scouting for over 50 years, is actively involved in a number of other local charities and is an energetic member of Epsom Methodist Church.

With 20 years experience in healthcare – from CAMHS to crisis mental health to dementia care – I’ve seen how underfunded, understaffed services fail our most vulnerable: children, adolescents, the elderly, the disabled. Too often, they face unacceptable waits or no support at all. As your Green Party councillor, I’ll fight for properly funded, accessible services, clean air to protect our health, and sensible development that includes schools, GPs, and green spaces—not just more housing. My healthcare experience means I know how to advocate, collaborate, and deliver. I want a community where no one is left behind.

My family and I moved to West Ewell three years ago, and it quickly became home. My son attends a local school, and this is the community where we want to see him grow up. Having been born in Birmingham and living across London for a decade, I’ve learned that communities are strongest when we welcome people, not scapegoat them. I’m a proud Trustee of Love Me Love My Mind, a wonderful local mental health charity, because supporting one another matters. As an accountant helping small businesses and people disadvantaged by the tax system, I know real change is needed — and that’s why I joined the Green Party.

Returning after his over decade-long absence, the previous National Vice Chairman of the Young Conservatives in the 1990s, Chris Muller prepares for his biggest battle yet: running for a seat in College Ward against the Residents Association. After dedicating himself to years of work in the field of education, teaching classics, and successfully leading a top girls' school, Chris comes back to the wider community to fight for his fellow citizens. A self-made man, growing up on the streets of Dartford, supporting himself from the age of 18 so he could attend university in London - he is conscious of the persistent and diligent mental motivation required to pursue success. With a strong passion for the importance of education and what the council needs, himself living in College Ward for over two decades, supporting and raising his family there - he is a strong advocate to ensure that College Ward, and the rest of Epsom and Ewell, develops stronger each year.

As a sitting Labour Councillor in Court Ward, I'm out every weekend knocking on doors helping residents with their problems. We get round the Ward every year or so and have really lifted the area.
We are itching to bring these roving surgeries to the new West Ewell Ward on East Surrey Council. Action speaks louder than words here.

After many successful years mastering the challenging and demanding sport of rowing, from winning events at Henley Woman's Regatta to receiving three blues for Oxford, Tracy enters her latest challenge battling for a different blue this time - the conservative seat in the Stoneleigh local election. A powerhouse of a woman, having held a number of jobs at a local school while raising two children, she really is someone who can do it all. With her personal and heartfelt appreciation to Stoneleigh for providing her grandparents and father an oasis of safety during the Second World War, in tandem with her renowned and unshakable 'get it done' attitude, Tracy Muller is the candidate for a safer and stronger Stoneleigh.

Hi I'm Alex.

I grew up in the local area and live in West Ewell with my wife and two daughters, who attend local schools.

Since 2018 I’ve been a borough councillor, representing part of West Ewell where the boundaries have been redrawn.

I’ve chosen to stand as an Independent rather than as a Residents’ Association candidate. I believe I can serve you better this way.

I hope you will think of the many years of hard work I’ve put in on litter, anti-social behaviour, fixing potholes, housing issues, parking problems and fighting crime here in West Ewell. There’s more to do.

I will give you a proven track record of delivery. I have the experience, knowledge and sheer willpower to make sure you get proper services from your council.

I campaigned successfully against the creation of a new parish council because I don’t want to see residents taxed even more. We already pay enough and 82% of residents agreed. We won!

Last year I bid for and won over £1m in funding for new playgrounds and sports facilities. Every child in Epsom & Ewell will have access to quality play areas no matter where they live. You will see these in West Ewell this summer.

Let’s take party politics out of local government. Vote Independent.

Paul is a retired solicitor. He grew up in a mining town in the North East and studied politics at University before going on to qualify as a lawyer. He has lived in Epsom for over 25 years and is proud to call it home. He is married and has one daughter. He is determined to work hard so as to ensure that the new East Surrey Council provides improved service delivery to all residents.

West Molesey is joining the new East Surrey Council and the election on Thursday 7th May is to elect your two councillors for the next five years. 
I am Steve Bax and have been a Molesey councillor since 2014, and serving the community for even longer. I am the current Deputy Cabinet Member Highways at Surrey County Council and the Mayor of Elmbridge for 2025-26.
I have earned a reputation for being a highly visible and active councillor who listens to residents and gets things done for Molesey. I always put community needs before party politics, as a good councillor should. 
I am proud to have secured funding for local groups and schools, new crossings outside St Lawrence School and St Albans, a new subsidised bus to Esher High School, grants for restoration work at Esher High and Cranmere Primary. I have funded throwlines at the riverside and a patrol boat and campaigned for free swimming at our leisure centres for teenagers in the summers holidays following tragic incidents on our river. 
I have secured £3m for the replacement of Mole Bridge and resurfaced local roads like Hurst Road and a mile of Walton Road. Resurfacing is the only sure way to stop potholes and I will be pushing for more roads funding for Molesey from the new council.
I will shortly be delivering a manifesto to every house detailing more of my plans and setting out a longer list of achievements. Everyone makes claims at election time but track record is the best indicator of what to expect.
West Molesey will only have two councillors at this new East Surrey Council and if they are not reliable or engaged, our area could lose out on its fair share of resources and go backwards. So this is an election about individuals - who is best for Molesey. It's not about parties or national politics.
Feel free to email me any questions and follow my public posts on Facebook. I hope you will vote with Molesey in mind on 7th May and vote for Cllr Steve Bax and my excellent running mate Sunny Cadman.

PS - I m

I am Sunny Cadman and I have lived in West Molesey for over 15 years, raising my family here and becoming deeply invested in our community.

Over recent years, I have been actively involved in local issues, working behind the scenes and on the ground to support residents, improve our environment and ensure our voices are heard. I believe being a councillor is about showing up, listening and taking action where it matters most.

I have supported local initiatives including tree planting and litter picking to enhance our green spaces, while also holding authorities to account when those efforts have not been properly protected or coordinated. I have supported residents on concerns such as road safety, parking pressures and the impact of overdevelopment in our neighbourhoods.

I am particularly passionate about safe routes for children. I have been actively pushing for a safe crossing between Grovelands Recreation playground to Hurst Park school (please sign my petition here: https://tinyurl.com/4u9umcjz) , recognising how important it is for families and school children to move around safely. Road safety improvements should not wait for accidents and I will continue to prioritise practical solutions that protect our community.

One issue I feel strongly about is the increasing number of family homes being intensified into high-occupancy HMOs without proper planning scrutiny. I will continue pushing for an Article 4 Direction for Molesey, ensuring that changes like these require full planning permission. (please sign my petition here: https://tinyurl.com/fwdtbhx7) This will help protect the character of our residential streets, reduce pressure on parking and infrastructure and give residents a stronger voice in shaping their area.

I am a committee member of the Molesey Garden Society and take pride in supporting local events and initiatives. This year, I will be volunteering at the Molesey Carnival, something I have attended every year. Supporting community events is

Stephen Bown -   Reform UK  Candidate - West Molesey Ward
I am Stephen Bown and am one of two Reform candidates, for West Molesey, in the East Surrey elections on May the 7th.  I have lived in Elmbridge near the Esher Molesey border since 1989.

I am not a career politician but recently became involved with politics because  I could no longer  watch the three main parties continue to mismanage  our country.  Over the past 25 years, they have done severe damage to our constitution, to our economy, to our businesses, to our culture and to our pockets.   I am determined to change that and be part of team and party that will fight to get the structural changes needed to re-float this great nation and protect its citizens and values.

I have had a long career in business and can see that we need real business experience in local government, as well as other skill sets, as it is no different to running a commercial business, however big or small.  Serve your customers well and they will value your service (our residents and local businesses), with constant improvement and innovation, whilst controlling and continuing to reduce costs so you can afford the services (your local taxes, which remember you have to pay after tax!).

I always viewed local elections to be about voting for local planning decisions, amenities and services that we residents wanted and could control.  That situation has now changed as central government has imposed its political will upon these services as well as demanding that we accommodate the failures of their immigration and economic policies.  Specific consequences include building on our green belt to accommodate a 20% increase in population over the past 20 years that is unmanageable at this scale.  We also have many decent hard working migrant families that contribute that feel the same pain.  We are now also expected to accept large numbers of unregulated HMOs to accommodate illegal undocumented  migrants, whilst the government claims succe

An assistant head teacher at a local primary school, Phil has lived with his wife in Elmbridge since 2011, first in Cobham and, since 2017, in Molesey. He has a particular passion for Computing and Science, and leads the subject and curriculum development across the school.

As a keen runner, he is worried by the declining quality and safety of our pavements and footpaths and the impact this is having on people with disabilities and those with mobility scooters who struggle to get to the shops safely. The protection of Elmbridge’s green areas, under threat from development, is also an issue which is close to his heart. If elected, he will work with Liberal Democrat Council colleagues to fight the Conservative government’s plans for ever more intensive development in our local area. He will also work towards increasing the number of electric car charging points, as well as addressing the provision of affordable homes.

In his spare time, Philip enjoys running, badminton and chess. He is a qualified coach in both badminton and chess.

Philip Stevenson's priorities - Improving pavements - Environment and protecting our green space - Affordable housing

I am not a career politician. I am a small business owner and Registered Manager in health and social care, working closely with people and supporting vulnerable individuals every day.
Running a small business has given me first-hand experience of the challenges faced by families, local services, and independent businesses.
I am standing because I believe our community deserves to be heard, supported, and represented with honesty.
If elected, I will listen to residents and act on real concerns, support local services and small businesses, promote accountability and transparency, and focus on practical solutions that make a real difference.
I am committed to being a strong and independent voice for our community.

Andy Dillon is a married father of three. More eco 'worrier' than 'warrior' and that has prompted the 54 year old to do more for the environment and green issues. 

Andy lives in Molesey, is a regular volunteer with the Surrey Wildlife Trust and a long standing member of the Woodland Trust for whom he has also volunteered. 

A sports journalist by trade, Andy wants to see top level football in particular become more sustainable.

I believe thriving local communities need councillors who listen, take action, and get things done for residents. Weybridge has been my home for over twenty years, and I care deeply about our town and the people who live here.

Since being elected Borough Councillor in 2023, I’ve brought energy, focus, and determination to the role, speaking up in the council chamber, scrutinising decisions, and working hard to resolve issues raised on the doorstep. One area that has been a persistent source of frustration for residents is highways, where I’ve been active on a range of issues. This includes challenging poorly managed roadworks that cause unnecessary congestion, engaging with large delivery companies to address the impact of overflow van parking, and co-leading the successful campaign to secure an HGV ban through Weybridge, improving safety and reducing heavy traffic.

Alongside this, I continue to work across a wide range of local issues that matter to residents. I’m committed to continuing that work and helping make Weybridge an even better place to live, work, and visit.

A local resident for over 15 years, I bring a background in healthcare leadership and a deep commitment to our community. Whether I’m rowing on the Thames, coaching, or leading local environmental projects, I’m driven by a desire to help our neighborhood thrive. My professional experience in complex problem-solving helps me navigate local challenges to deliver practical, positive results for my fellow residents.

We are fortunate to live in an attractive part of the country and I want to keep it that way.  Our area is under a lot of pressure to accept new development. Whilst there should be homes for local people, this needs to be managed. New homes and more people means more traffic. This has become a significant issue in many local roads. Speed of vehicles, parking and congestion are daily battles. Working with the ward’s other Councillors, I will press forcefully for these and other concerns in Oatlands and Burwood Park to be given the attention they deserve. Local services are also under pressure and your Councillors must be able to rise to the challenges this brings. The chaos which ensued following the botched introduction of the new waste and recycling service by the then Liberal Democrat/Residents’ Association coalition demonstrated the need for focussed and capable leadership. My time as a Councillor in another authority taught me how to work with officers to plan and work within local government budgets. As a former Councillor and in my professional work with Members of Parliament, I have organised and led projects, worked with people from a wide variety of backgrounds and managed large teams of volunteers. I cannot promise to solve the concerns of everyone in Oatlands and Burwood Park but I will be responsive to and supportive of residents and represent you in the best way possible. I have lived in Oatlands for over twenty years and wish not only to maintain and enhance the quality of life many of us enjoy but also improve life for those less fortunate.

Honest, Hardworking & Caring. Working to maintain and enhance the quality of life so many of us enjoy in Weybridge and improve life for those less fortunate.

My choice in becoming a Green Party candidate stems from my dual roles as an educator and a parent. As a teacher with over fifteen years of experience, I have cultivated a deep understanding of the educational system and the importance of sustainable practices in shaping future generations. I am also deeply aware of the issues young people are personally impacted by in today’s society. Additionally, as a mother, I am acutely aware of the challenges families face in today’s world, particularly concerning climate change and its impact on our children’s future. This personal connection drives my commitment to advocating for policies that prioritise ecological sustainability, equality, and community well-being, making me a passionate and effective representative for the Green Party.

I’ve lived in Weybridge for 10 years, and as one of the mangers for Shooting Star Children’s Hospices, I help run vital fundraising activities in the community to support local families with children with life limiting conditions. I Chair the Weybridge Independent Network, a group of small locally businesses whose aim is to support each other and the community. Day to day, I see the need for well-funded vital public services to support families in hardship. Local business is essential to the recovery from the pandemic, and we need policies from all levels of government which champion their success.
